rspStmt.AuthEnty: Research on Open Educational Resources for Development (ROER4D)

verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: Version 1 of the dataset was deposited with DataFirst on 16 September 2015. Version 1.1 has been anonymised further.

stdyInfo.abstract: The Research on Open Educational Resources for Development (ROER4D) project is based at the Centre for Innovation in Learning and Teaching, University of Cape Town, South Africa. Glenda Cox and Henry Trotter of the Centre for Innovation in Learning and Teaching at the University of Cape Town undertook Sub-project 4 of ROER4D. The project, Research into the Social and Cultural Acceptability of Open Educational Resources in South Africa, aims to describe and explain the barriers to and enablers of Open Educational Resources (OER) contribution at institutions in South Africa. The key objective of the research was to understand why scholars contribute or refuse to contribute their teaching materials as OER. The project utilised a mixed methods approach. Surveys of and interviews with academics at three South African institutions were conducted in March 2015 to understand the conditions under which the contribution and/or use of OER would be considered socially and culturally acceptable. Focusing on academics’ teaching practices at the University of Cape Town (UCT) (urban, contact), the University of South Africa (UNISA) (distance, online/ correspondence), and University of Fort Hare University (rural, contact). The resultant dataset includes the survey (quantitative) and interview (qualitative) data.

sumDscr.geogCover: The surveys were conducted at three universities in South Africa and the data is not representative of South Africa as a whole.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation of the data is individual university.
sumDscr.universe: The survey covered academicand support staff at the three universities in the study.

stdyInfo.notes: The research collected data on why academics contribute or refuse to contribute their teaching materials as Open Educational Resources.

dataColl.collSitu: The interviews were conducted with 18 people (6 per university), and comprised 50-56 questions and lasted between 30 minutes and 1 hour. The online survey collected quantitative data while open-ended interviews were undertaken at the workshops.

anlyInfo.dataAppr: Age data was collected for age groups. However, there was an overlap between age groups 56-60 and 60-65 in the survey instrument. Respondents for these groups were therefore combined into a 56-65 age group.

useStmt.citReq: Research on Open Educational Resources for Development. Open Educational Resources in South Africa 2015. (ROER4D Sub-project 4) [dataset]. Version 1.1. Cape Town: ROER4D [producer], 2015. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2015. 10.25828/b9a8-yv68
useStmt.conditions: Public use data, available to all

var.name: resp_id
var.labl: Respondent identifier

var.name: q1_insti
var.labl: Institution

var.name: q2_gender
var.labl: Gender

var.name: q3_age
var.labl: Age

var.name: q4_desig
var.labl: Designation

var.name: q4_desig_o
var.labl: Designation - other

var.name: q5_discip
var.labl: Major discipline

var.name: q6_educ
var.labl: Highest educational qualification

var.name: q7_yrsteach
var.labl: Years of teaching experience

var.name: q8_jobface
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - face-to-face teaching

var.name: q8_jobdist
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - distance education

var.name: q8_jobonline
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - online teaching/facilitation

var.name: q8_jobblend
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - blended/hybrid (face-to-face and distance/online)

var.name: q8_jobworkbased
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - work-based training

var.name: q8_jobresearch
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - research

var.name: q8_jobmanage
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - management

var.name: q8_jobother
var.labl: Nature of your job includes - other

var.name: q9_lang1
var.labl: Most common language used in teaching

var.name: q9_lang2
var.labl: Most common language used in teaching

var.name: q9_lang3
var.labl: Most common language used in teaching

var.name: q10_used
var.labl: Used OER previously

var.name: q11_contr
var.labl: Contributed OER previously

var.name: qb_attit1
var.labl: Sharing of educational resources improves my professional respect

var.name: qb_attit2
var.labl: It gives me pleasure if someone adopts/adapts my educational resources

var.name: qb_attit3
var.labl: Sharing helps me to get feedback

var.name: qb_attit4
var.labl: Sharing enhances my personal and organizational reputation

var.name: qb_attit5
var.labl: Sharing of educational resources increases my profile amongst peers and others

var.name: qb_attit6
var.labl: OER increases my network and sphere of influence

var.name: qb_attit7
var.labl: As a teacher, it is my responsibility to share all educational resources create

var.name: qb_attit8
var.labl: OER improves my chance of recognition at a global level

var.name: qb_attit9
var.labl: I believe that sharing educational materials as OER will encourage others to do

var.name: qb_attit10
var.labl: Sharing enhances my confidence as I see myself in part of larger community

var.name: qb_attit11
var.labl: When others use my OER, it improves my sense of achievement

var.name: qb_attit12
var.labl: OER helps to disseminate my ideas

var.name: qb_attit13
var.labl: OER promotes collaboration and consortia

var.name: qb_attit14
var.labl: I have knowledge of Intellectual Property Rights to understand OER

var.name: qb_attit15
var.labl: I am efficient in Information Communication Technology (ICT) skills to adopt and

var.name: qb_attit16
var.labl: I adopt OER for my teaching as they fulfill academic requirement of my students

var.name: qb_attit17
var.labl: My own competencies and knowledge towards OER help me to participate or adopt OE

var.name: qc_good
var.labl: I believe that OER are â€˜goodâ€™ for people as it improves their learning.

var.name: qc_give
var.labl: I try to contribute to OER to give back to society

var.name: qc_involved
var.labl: I like to be involved in peer production of OER

var.name: qc_reach
var.labl: Through OER, I can reach disadvantaged communities

var.name: qc_access
var.labl: OER will help developing countries increase access to education

var.name: qc_basic
var.labl: Sharing knowledge is a basic academic value

var.name: qc_innate
var.labl: OER caters to innate desire to learn, improve and progress

var.name: qc_opportunities
var.labl: OER gives me opportunities to learn new things

var.name: qc_saves
var.labl: OER saves my time

var.name: qc_lessexp
var.labl: OER is less expensive

var.name: qc_bestmaterial
var.labl: OER provides access to best materials and teachers

var.name: qc_selfconf
var.labl: OER increases my self-confidence

var.name: qc_recognition
var.labl: Involvement in OER will give me recognition

var.name: qc_image
var.labl: OER improves professional image

var.name: qc_credit
var.labl: Receiving appropriate credit will help me uptake OER

var.name: qc_feedback
var.labl: I like receiving comments and feedbacks from experts and seniors on OER created

var.name: qc_partnerships
var.labl: OER provides us opportunities for establishing new partnerships

var.name: qc_easy
var.labl: Technology associated with OER is easy

var.name: qc_ipr
var.labl: I know about my Intellectual Property Rights under Creative Commons licenses

var.name: qd_saves
var.labl: OER saves time of the teachers

var.name: qd_cost
var.labl: OERs brings down the cost of learning materials

var.name: qd_free
var.labl: OERs are free resources available in open license

var.name: qd_permission
var.labl: I do not need permission to re-use OER

var.name: qd_fulfil
var.labl: I often use OER which fulfill the pedagogical need of teachingâ€“learning proc

var.name: qd_appropriate
var.labl: If OERs are appropriate in their content I prefer to use them

var.name: qd_trustworthy
var.labl: I prefer to use OER from trustworthy sources

var.name: qd_quality
var.labl: Open license of OER enables continuous quality improvement

var.name: qd_reputed
var.labl: I use trustworthy OER from reputed institutions

var.name: qd_peer
var.labl: Lack of peer review of OERs make them susceptible to poor quality

var.name: qd_assist
var.labl: OERs assist the developing countries to have quality materials

var.name: qd_questionnable
var.labl: Quality of OER is questionable

var.name: qd_localization
var.labl: OER needs localization

var.name: qe_barrier_ipr
var.labl: Lack of understanding of Intellectual Property licenses, Copyrights and Creative

var.name: qe_rank_ipr
var.labl: Lack of understanding of Intellectual Property licenses, Copyrights and Creative

var.name: qe_barrier_knowledge
var.labl: Lack of knowledge for using OER in my teaching and learning process - Select

var.name: qe_rank_knowledge
var.labl: Lack of knowledge for using OER in my teaching and learning process - Rank

var.labl: Lack of recognition and rewards system for developing OER - Select

var.name: qe_rank_recogn
var.labl: Lack of recognition and rewards system for developing OER - Rank

var.labl: Lack of financial resources by institution to invest in OER - Select

var.name: qe_rank_financ
var.labl: Lack of financial resources by institution to invest in OER - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_support
var.labl: Lack of technological support to resolve my problems - Select

var.name: qe_rank_support
var.labl: Lack of technological support to resolve my problems - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_ability
var.labl: In-ability to find existing OER on topics of my interest - Select

var.name: qe_rank_ability
var.labl: In-ability to find existing OER on topics of my interest - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_confi
var.labl: Lack of confidence about the quality of my work - Select

var.name: qe_rank_confi
var.labl: Lack of confidence about the quality of my work - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_incompat
var.labl: Incompatibility of OER to my university Learning Management System (LMS) - Selec

var.name: qe_rank_incompat
var.labl: Incompatibility of OER to my university Learning Management System (LMS) - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_diff
var.labl: Difficulty to remix OER for specific users - Select

var.name: qe_rank_diff
var.labl: Difficulty to remix OER for specific users - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_availa
var.labl: Unavailability of OER in native language - Select

var.name: qe_rank_availa
var.labl: Unavailability of OER in native language - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_skills
var.labl: Lack of ICT skills required to create OER - Select

var.name: qe_rank_skills
var.labl: Lack of ICT skills required to create OER - Rank

var.labl: Lack of Institutional policy on OER - Select

var.name: qe_rank_policy
var.labl: Lack of Institutional policy on OER - Rank

var.labl: Non-user friendly OER platforms - Select

var.labl: Non-user friendly OER platforms - Rank

var.labl: Poor technical infrastructure - Select

var.labl: Poor technical infrastructure - Rank

var.labl: Difficulty in collaboration - Select

var.name: qe_rank_coll
var.labl: Difficulty in collaboration - Rank

var.labl: Inadequate bandwidth - Select

var.name: qe_rank_bandw
var.labl: Inadequate bandwidth - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_workl
var.labl: Current workload - Select

var.name: qe_rank_workl
var.labl: Current workload - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_time
var.labl: Lack of time - Select

var.name: qe_rank_time
var.labl: Lack of time - Rank

var.name: qe_barrier_o
var.labl: Other barriers

catgry.catValu: Training on creation and use of OER

var.name: other
var.labl: Other notes

catgry.catValu: I am more interesting in creating rather than using for teaching, but I do share OERs I find with people I think may find these resources useful

My interest is as a UNISA Press publisher, resporting to the Research Department. High value is placed on commercial exploitation of, ie. journals (and ther is still a lot of fear around loss of income) for a puboisher. I am keen to develop a h
Questions of suitability for South African curricula.
The authenticity of the information provided.
The capability of using OER / personal attributes
The course was very good, thank you!
These questions are hard to complete for a student
